title: KASP Genotyping of Wheat Stripe Rust Resistance Loci
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4578f","text":"This protocol describes the Kompetitive Allele-Specific PCR (KASP) assay used to genotype the wheat stripe rust resistance locus QYr.hzau-2BS. The assay was applied to a population of 153 F₅ recombinant inbred lines (RILs) derived from a cross between the susceptible parent 'Apav#1' (CIMMYT GID 1854090) and the resistant parent 'Kijil' (CIMMYT GID 6342979). Polymorphic markers, including KASP_2BS, were used to genotype the entire RIL population following conventional PCR amplification protocols.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":129,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"88v83","text":"Ensure all reagents (KASP Master Mix, primers, DNA) are completely thawed and mixed well. Equilibrate all reagents to room temperature before starting. Prepare the PCR plate and centrifuge briefly before dispensing samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"fmgdj","text":"Caution:​ KASP assays are highly sensitive to contamination. Use filter tips and maintain a clean work area. Avoid cross-contamination between samples, especially when handling small volumes (\u003c 5 µL).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: PCR Master Mix Preparation Prepare the reaction mix on ice according to the table above. Ensure thorough mixing by gentle pipetting or brief centrifugation.

Thermal Cycling Conditions Perform PCR amplification using the following program: Pre-denaturation:​ 95°C for 5 minutes Amplification (35 cycles): 95°C for 1 minute 58°C for 20 seconds 72°C for 45 seconds Final Extension:​ 72°C for 10 minutes Hold:​ 4°C
Genotype Calling Analyze the PCR products using real-time PCR (RT-PCR) to determine the allelic status based on fluorescence signals (FAM/HEX channels).
creator: Shanshan Yan
